top_posts.text: I built a Threads account that writes, posts, and improves without me touching it. It uses the official Meta API. Free tools. One-time setup. And yes, you’re reading a post it wrote right now.

Most creators waste hours manually posting every day. Instead, build a feedback loop:  
Post → Read analytics → Let AI rewrite tomorrow’s content based on what worked.  

I set this up once with free tools (GitHub Actions + free AI APIs). It now runs itself. No daily grind.  

That’s the whole trick. One setup, zero upkeep.

4 things nobody tells you about hands-free Threads growth:

1. The official Meta API is the difference between “automation that works” and “automation that gets banned.”
2. Free tools (GitHub Actions + free AI APIs) run everything — no SaaS bill, no subscription creep.
3. The real magic isn’t posting. It’s the daily analytics loop: read what worked, write better tomorrow.
4. Tokens auto-refresh. You set it up once and genuinely never log in again.

Automation gets you banned" is the most repeated myth in Threads growth.

Here's the reality: the official Meta Threads API exists for exactly this. It's the same permission system brands like Nike and Netflix use. No browser hacks. No fake bot behavior. Just proper API calls that Meta explicitly supports.

The system running this account uses that API. It posts, reads analytics, and improves — all within Meta's rules. Tokens even refresh themselves.

Myth busted. Now you can build...

I haven't touched this account in 3 months. It still posts every day. Gets better every week.

The secret isn't willpower. It's one afternoon of setup using free tools and the official Meta API. The system writes, posts, reads analytics, and rewrites tomorrow's posts — all on its own. Even refreshes its own tokens.

Consistency without the burnout. That's the whole game.
